name: Hainaut (Belgium)

colors.symbolism: the heraldic gold of the historic arms of the County of Hainaut
the two black lions, after the arms of the County of Flanders
the two red lions, after the arms of the County of Holland; also the tongues and claws of the black lions

description: A banner of the historic arms of Hainaut: a yellow field quartered by lions — black lions with red tongues and claws in the first and fourth quarters, red lions with blue tongues and claws in the second and third.
history: Hainaut has no officially adopted provincial flag; in practice a banner of the historic arms of the medieval County of Hainaut is used. The quartering combines the arms of Flanders (black lion on gold) and Holland (red lion on gold), reflecting the centuries-long personal union under the Counts of Hainaut, who were also Counts of Holland. Honorary flags of the Governor based on the same arms were adopted by Council Order on 28 October 1936.

features.symbolism: all four quarters on a gold field

features.role: two black lions rampant with red tongues and claws, in the upper-hoist and lower-fly quarters (the arms of the County of Flanders)
features.symbolism: the Flemish lion, reflecting the Hainaut counts' Flemish heritage

features.role: two red lions rampant with blue tongues and claws, in the upper-fly and lower-hoist quarters (the arms of the County of Holland)
features.symbolism: the Dutch lion, reflecting the personal union of the Counts of Hainaut and Counts of Holland
